page: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. The system features 192 virtual CPUs mapped across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with 11400 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-core CPU stress tests, Passmark CPU scores, and software compilation. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only bandwidth, reaching 15491.12 GB/sec for cached blocks and 585.22 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, with Redis achieving 240.29 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and the Passmark memory score align with the middle 50% average.

Given its massive memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong multi-threaded execution, this bare-metal instance is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring dense resource consolidation without hypervisor overhead. It is optimized for large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores, high-throughput data processing, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) further supports matrix-math intensive workloads, such as CPU-based deep learning and prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 192 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, database operations, and memory bandwidth, as evidenced by its top 10% ranking in multi-core stress tests and Redis SET operations. While single-core performance remains in the average tier, its massive memory footprint and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive applications. The platform also supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enhancing its capability for CPU-based machine learning inference and cryptographic tasks without requiring dedicated GPU hardware.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 11400 GB of NVMe SSD storage, optimized for large-scale databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory, local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core processing for demanding enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
1536 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and heavy data processing
